sentence: If she had studied harder, she would have passed the exam
hint: Third conditional: If + had + pp, would have + pp
explanation: Third conditional: If + past perfect, would have + past participle. Never 'would had'.

sentence: I wish I had known about the deadline earlier
hint: Wish + past perfect for past regrets
explanation: Wish + past perfect for regrets about the past: 'wish I had known'.

sentence: If they had left earlier, they would not have missed the flight
explanation: Third conditional negative: 'would not have missed'.

sentence: I wish I could speak Japanese fluently
hint: Wish + could for present wishes about ability
explanation: Wish + could + base form for wishes about present ability.

sentence: If I had accepted that job, I would be living in London now
hint: Mixed conditional: past condition, present result
explanation: Mixed conditional: If + past perfect (past), would + be + -ing (present result).

sentence: If only I had listened to my parents' advice
explanation: 'If only' + past perfect for strong regret about the past.

sentence: He would have been promoted if he had worked harder
explanation: Third conditional with result first. 'Would have been' (not 'would had been').

sentence: I wish people would stop using so much plastic
hint: Wish + would for complaints about others
explanation: Wish + would for annoyance or complaints about other people's behavior.

sentence: If we had not taken that shortcut, we would still be lost
explanation: Mixed conditional: past condition (had not taken), present result (would be lost).

sentence: She wishes she had not said those things to him
explanation: Wish + past perfect negative for regretting past actions.

sentence: If he were more confident, he would have applied for the position
hint: Mixed conditional: present condition, past result
explanation: Mixed: If + were (present), would have + pp (past result). Use 'were' not 'was'.

sentence: I wish I did not have to work this weekend
explanation: Wish + past simple for present wishes: 'wish I didn't have to'.

sentence: If they had invested in that company, they would be rich now
explanation: Mixed conditional: past condition, present result.

sentence: If only she had told us the truth from the beginning
explanation: 'If only' + past perfect for strong past regret.

sentence: We would not have gotten lost if we had used the map
explanation: Third conditional negative with result first. 'Would not have' (never 'would had').

sentence: I wish the weather were better today
explanation: Wish + were (subjunctive) for present wishes. Formal: 'were' not 'was'.

sentence: If she had not missed the interview, she might have gotten the job
explanation: Third conditional with 'might' instead of 'would' for less certainty.

sentence: He wishes he had traveled more when he was younger
explanation: Wish + past perfect for past regret with time clause.

sentence: If I had known you were coming, I would have prepared dinner
explanation: Third conditional with embedded clause: 'had known you were coming'.

sentence: I wish I were taller so I could reach the top shelf
explanation: Wish + were for present wish + 'so' clause for purpose.

sentence: If we had booked the tickets earlier, they would have been cheaper
explanation: Third conditional: past perfect condition, would have + pp result.

sentence: She wishes her neighbors would not play loud music at night
explanation: Wish + would for complaints about others' behavior.

sentence: If only we had more time to finish the project
explanation: 'If only' + past simple for present wishes (like 'wish').

sentence: If he had not been so careless, the accident would not have happened
explanation: Third conditional: double negative — 'had not been' and 'would not have happened'.

sentence: I wish I had started learning English when I was a child
explanation: Wish + past perfect for past regret with time clause.
